mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when either series declines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output utilization, which directly translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85%, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% month-over-month forces anticipated monetary tightening and erodes real consumer purchasing power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal reverses direction when core inflation remains stable.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and the consumer price index signals restrictive real interest rates that suppress aggregate demand and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate differential shows no correlation with 60-day returns, or positive returns dominate when the gap exceeds 2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls combined with stable unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive returns when unemployment simultaneously rises, or the signal loses significance during structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production and gross domestic product growth rates indicate strengthening economic output that immediately boosts equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias during output expansions, or the signal consistently underperforms during periods of supply chain disruption.
